turns.student: How have you been feeling overall lately?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been feeling pretty unwell. My energy levels have been really low, and I've just been feeling tired all the time.

turns.student: Tell me about your day-to-day — how has your health been in general?
turns.patient: Honestly, it's gotten progressively worse over the past week or so. I've had this persistent cough that started dry and became productive with yellowish-green sputum over the last 5 days.

turns.student: You mentioned some health issues. Can you walk me through what happens?
turns.patient: The cough started as a dry, annoying one, but it got worse and now it's really bothering me. It's been productive with yellowish-green sputum for about 5 days now, and it's still there. I've tried taking OTC dextromethorphan to help suppress it, but it hasn't brought much relief. When I cough, it feels like my whole chest is involved – not just my throat or lungs, if that makes sense.

turns.student: And when these episodes occur, what does your body do?
turns.patient: When I have one of these episodes, my body really tightens up and I feel like I'm having trouble breathing. It's hard to catch my breath and sometimes I wheeze a bit afterwards.

turns.student: Let's focus on the episodes. Walk me through one from start to finish.
turns.patient: Okay, so... when an episode starts, it usually begins with a dry cough that can feel really scratchy in my throat. Sometimes I'll try to clear my throat or cough to get rid of it, but that just makes it worse and brings up the yellowish-green sputum. It's like my body is trying to expel something, you know? After I cough up some mucus, I can feel a bit better for a short while, but then the coughing starts again, often accompanied by wheezing and difficulty breathing. The episodes usually happen when I'm lying down or after exertion, and they can be quite severe – it feels like my whole chest is involved and I get this tightness in my lungs that's hard to describe.

turns.student: During an episode, do you notice anything besides the main symptom?
turns.patient: When I'm having one of these episodes, I also feel a bit lightheaded and I might break out in a light sweat. It's like my body is really working hard to try and get rid of whatever's causing the cough, you know?

turns.student: Specifically, any changes in your skin — like sweating or flushing?
turns.patient: Actually, I've been having night sweats. For 4 nights now, I've woken up with my pajamas completely soaked through, which is really unsettling.

turns.student: And your stomach — any queasiness or nausea during these moments?
turns.patient: Actually, to be honest, I haven't had any queasiness or nausea specifically related to the coughing episodes. But what I have noticed is that my appetite has been really off lately – I've been feeling kind of nauseous in a general sense, and I've lost interest in eating altogether for about 5 days now.

turns.student: What about other parts of your body? Any pain, tingling, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: Honestly, besides the coughing episodes and night sweats, I haven't noticed any other unusual sensations. However, my low-grade fever has been a bit of an issue – it's around 38.2°C (100.8°F) most evenings, but it's intermittent. It's not severe or anything, but it's definitely there.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: Yes, I've been having a low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), that's worse in the evenings. And yes, I've also been experiencing night sweats - for 4 nights now, I've woken up with my pajamas completely soaked through.

turns.student: Now let's zoom out. Any swelling you've noticed anywhere on your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really noticed any swelling anywhere on my body. But to be clear, that doesn't mean it couldn't be there - I'm not always paying attention to every detail of my own body. Can we go back to the coughing episodes for a second? You asked earlier about wheezing and difficulty breathing during those episodes...

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your weight or appetite?
turns.patient: To be honest, my appetite has been really off lately. I'd say it's decreased significantly over the past 5 days. I just haven't felt like eating much at all, and even when I do try to eat, I don't feel hungry for long.

turns.student: Let me ask about your medical history. What conditions have you been treated for?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've had seasonal allergies since I was a teenager, and I take over-the-counter cetirizine when my symptoms flare up. Besides that, I don't really have any other significant medical issues or conditions to speak of.

turns.student: Any medications you're taking? Are you taking them consistently?
turns.patient: I've been taking cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season. Recently, I've also taken some over-the-counter dextromethorphan for my cough, but like I mentioned earlier, it hasn't brought much relief.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle. How active are you?
turns.patient: I'm a teacher at an elementary school, so I'm on my feet most of the day. I'd say I get a moderate amount of physical activity, but it's not like I'm exercising or anything. Just running around after 30 kids all day is enough to keep me moving! Lately, though, even that's been feeling exhausting.

turns.student: And your diet — what does a typical day of eating look like?
turns.patient: To be honest, my appetite has been really off lately. I've just not felt like eating much at all, and even when I do try to eat, I don't feel hungry for long. For the past 5 days or so, I've probably eaten about half of what I normally would in a day.

turns.student: How about alcohol or smoking?
turns.patient: I do drink socially, but it's just on weekends and it's usually one to two glasses of wine. And no, I've never smoked.

turns.student: Now family history. Any medical problems run in your family?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, which is something she's had for a long time. She's always been careful with her breathing and takes medication to manage it when needed.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious or chronic conditions?
turns.patient: Actually, my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer. I'm not sure if that's relevant, but I just remembered it now.

turns.student: We've covered a lot. Is there anything we haven't discussed that you think is relevant?
turns.patient: Actually, one thing has been bothering me, and I'm not even sure if it's related to my symptoms, but... several students in my class have come down with upper respiratory infections over the past month. I've noticed that we all tend to get sick around this time of year, but this season seems particularly bad. I don't know if that means anything or not, but it might be worth mentioning, right?
